name: Aker
description: Aker is an ancient Egyptian earth deity, typically depicted as two lions facing away from each other, representing the eastern and western horizons or gateways through which the sun god Ra passed each night. He is associated with the protection of the sun god during his nightly journey through the underworld, ensuring safe passage and rebirth at dawn. Aker is also linked to the concept of the earth's boundaries and the transition between day and night, life and death. He guards the entrance and exit to the underworld, preventing dangerous entities from escaping and ensuring the sun's safe return. The lions often have the sun disc between them, symbolizing the rising sun. Aker's role emphasizes his protective nature and his critical function in the cyclical renewal of life. He embodies the stability of the earth and the power to ward off evil forces, ensuring the cosmic order is maintained. While not as widely worshipped as other major Egyptian deities, Aker's presence reflects the Egyptian understanding of the earth as a protective barrier and a crucial element in the sun's journey.
subtitle: Guardian of the horizons and underworld gates

keyMyths.title: Aker's Protection of Ra
keyMyths.description: Aker protects Ra as he travels through the underworld each night. He prevents the serpent Apophis from attacking the sun god.

keyMyths.title: Guarding the Eastern and Western Gates
keyMyths.description: Aker is depicted as guarding the eastern and western gates of the underworld. He allows the sun to rise and set, ensuring the cycle of day and night.

keyMyths.title: The Lions of Yesterday and Tomorrow
keyMyths.description: The two lions representing Aker symbolize yesterday and tomorrow. They represent the past and future, and the continuous cycle of time.
